dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 5 responding for possible seizures.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What seems to be the problem today?
I think I had a seizure, or maybe something worse. I was walking and then I woke up on the ground.
Mr. Thomas, my name is Steve. Can you tell me your name?
My name is Fred Thomas.
I've had seizures before when I stop drinking-like today. I haven't had a drink in about six hours and then I think that triggered this.
You also mentioned taking some IV heroin before calling 911, correct?
Mr. Thomas, are you in any pain currently?
No, I'm not in any pain. I just have the weakness and a headache.
What medications are you currently taking?
Mr. Thomas, we noted you are alert and oriented in all four areas (AOx4). GCS is 15. Would you like help with anything?

Glucose was 94 initially. There are no obvious signs of trauma and no pain reported by you. However, you have a history of alcohol dependence and opioid use. Have you seen these symptoms before in relation to either of these issues?
Glucose was 94 initially. There are no obvious signs of trauma and no pain reported by you. We observed some slurred speech and left-sided weakness.
Yeah, no actual pain. Just flashing lights, some weakness, and I had trouble moving my left side after the episode.
Our monitor shows a blood pressure of 119 over 80 and a pulse of 113. Respirations are 16.
Your oxygen saturation is 92% on room air.
The EKG is showing sinus tachycardia.
We attempted an IV into an extremity vein for access, but we were unsuccessful due to poor peripheral circulation.
Fred, your oxygen is now 95% and your pulse is at 104 with blood pressure at 107 over 69. Your latest EKG shows sinus rhythm.
Fred, I want to check your feet and hands for sensation and movement. You mentioned some weakness-can we test that?
Left side is worse. I had left-sided facial drooping and trouble moving it after the incident.
Blood pressure is now 109 over 72, with a pulse of 109 and respirations up to 24.
Oxygen saturation is down to 93% on room air.
Fred, you mentioned flashing lights. Have you had a headache?
Yes, I started seeing flashing lights just before I lost it. I also had a severe headache.
We later took another glucose reading, but it was unreadable. Have you had full use of your left side at any time since the incident?
No, not really. I still can't feel it much and it feels paralyzed. I have numbness and tingling throughout my left side.
Your respiratory rate is now 18.
On a scale of one to ten, how severe is your pain right now?
I don't feel any pain, really. Just the headache and weakness are noticeable.
Pale color and pallor were noted during examination, consistent with your reported symptoms.
You also reported left-sided weakness and facial droop post-incident. Is there any numbness or tingling with that?
Additionally, have you ever experienced true paralysis or complete loss of movement from this phenomenon before?
No, it's never been this severe. I think this makes me more concerned. It's the complete deadness on my left side.
We are following protocols for seizure precautions and TIA evaluation due to your symptoms and medical history.
We are also concerned about a possible intracranial event due to the flashing lights and new-onset neurological deficits.
Additionally, due to your reported headache and flashing lights prior to the episode, we are also considering the possibility of a migraine-related event.
Fred, because of your left-sided weakness, episode with flashing lights, headache, and stroke-like symptoms, we are upgrading to hot transport for quick evaluation at the hospital.
Unit 5, requesting a hot transport to [COMPANY_NAME], reasons include TIA concern and stroke protocol activation.
